{{otheruses|Shinto Shrine in Numazu, Shizuoka Prefecture|Shinto Shrine in Mishima, Shizuoka Prefecture|Yanagihara Shrine (Mishima City)}} {{Shinto Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) keidai.JPG|260px]]
Main Shrine Worship Hall (left) and Sessha Yoshida Shrine (right) |Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|12=draft|12=draft}} Miwaku 335 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|8.1|N|138|52|16.4|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=楊原神社}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft|12=draft}})
Formerly {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = |Fuda and such = |Annual Festival = {{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka#Japan|label=楊原神社|lat_deg=35|lat_min=5|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=138|lon_min=52|lon_sec=16.4|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=220|caption=}} {{Infobox mapframe|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-width=220}} }} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Torii
]] '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft|12=draft}}), and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja|旧社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == '''Principal Enshrined Deity''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ōyamatsumi-kami)Deity information is based on the shrine's historical records. '''Auxiliary Enshrined Deities''' * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Stone-Long Princess Deity|lt_ja|石長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwanagahime-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Tree-Flower Blooming Princess Deity|lt_ja|木花開耶姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto) == History == === Overview === The founding period is unknown. Prior to its relocation to the current site during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is said that the shrine was enshrined at the location of Yanagihara in Numazu City (approximately 500 m to the southeast of the present site).Shrine historical records. The earliest mention in national history appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}'', which records that on January 17 of the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade accompanied by a bestowment of a certain {{ill|Kami Ranking|ja|神階|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}'' {{ill|Divine Name List of Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Divine Name List|lt_ja|神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded in {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tagata District|ja|田方郡|12=draft|12=draft}} as "Yanagihara Shrine Myōjin Taisha", listing it as the only {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tagata District.From "Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture", Shimokounuki Village section.Note: Although printed editions list this shrine as Myōjin Taisha, the Kujō family manuscripts designate the Iwayo-no-mikoto Shrine as Myōjin Taisha instead (see "History of Shizuoka Prefecture" [Yamakawa Publishing] p. 50). Also, the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'' contains an entry "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin". However, since the left bank of the Kano River—including both the present shrine grounds and the former site (Yanagihara in Numazu City)—has belonged to Suruga Province at least since the early modern period, doubts have been raised regarding its classification as Myōjin Taisha. Regarding this, given that the place name "Yanagihara" has persisted and numerous archaeological finds from the surrounding area indicate the reasonable enshrinement of a Myōjin Taisha here, there is a theory that a border shift between Suruga and Izu occurred after the Engishiki. In the past, the parish area comprised Uekounuki Village, Shimokounuki Village, Zentaifu, and Wareidō, and the shrine was also known as "Ōmiya Daimyōjin", "Kounuki Daimyōjin", and "Matsuhiko Daimyōjin". During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|12=draft|12=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|12=draft|12=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft|12=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Ooasa Shrine (Shimokounuki, Numazu City)
Possibly the mountain shrine of Yanagihara Shrine.]] ; Ooasa Shrine :* Location: Numazu City, Shimokounuki, Yamamiya-mae 3056-2 ({{Coord|35|04|25.83|N|138|51|52.74|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Ooasa Shrine (possibly mountain shrine)}}) :* Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ōhirume-no-mikoto|ja|大日孁命|lt=Great Sun Goddess|lt_ja|おおひるめのみこと|12=draft|12=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} "Ooasa Shrine" branch shrine, formerly {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} :* Annual Festival: January 16 : It is known as "Ooasa Shrine". It is located approximately 1.5 km southwest of Yanagihara Shrine at the southern foot of Ushinobeyama facing the sea. It shares the same enshrinement tradition and content as Yanagihara Shrine, and is considered to be one entity. It is speculated that there once was a shoal between it and Yanagihara Shrine, and that this shrine was originally enshrined on a small island in the sea as a mountain shrine. Furthermore, the designated branch shrine of the Shikinaisha "Ooasa Shrine" is not this shrine but rather {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine (Izu City)|ja|大宮神社 (伊豆市)|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt_ja|大宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in Kamishiroiwa, Izu City), which is more strongly regarded.See "The Gods of Japan", section on Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|
{{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|12=draft|12=draft}} Kitatanamachi)
One theory identifies it as Izu Province's Miya. There are various theories regarding its relationship with Yanagihara Shrine of Numazu.]] ; {{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} :* Location: {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kitatanamachi 4-7 ({{Coord|35|7|4.80|N|138|55|5.84|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Yanagihara Shrine (Possibly the remote worship office of the former provincial capital)}}) :* Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: Either as recorded in the Kami Ranking List as "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin" or as the Izu Province's Miya{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=559}} : It is known as "Yanagihara Shrine". It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (December 31) == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimokounuki Miwaku 335 '''Transportation Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company (JR Central)|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Central) {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|Gotemba Line|ja|御殿場線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Numazu Station|ja|沼津駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes on foot) ** Bus and rental bicycle available == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{reflist|group=注}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Records * {{Cite book|和書|editor=秋山章纂修、萩原正平増訂|author=|year=|title=増訂豆州志稿 巻八上|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|増訂豆州志稿 巻八上}}}} ** 東海|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022208|ref={{Harvid|日本の神々|1987}}}} == Related Topics == * {{commonscat-inline|Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu)}} == External Links == * Prior to its relocation to the current site during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is said that the shrine was enshrined at the location of Yanagihara in Numazu City (approximately 500 m to the southeast of the present site).Shrine historical records. In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' {{ill|Divine Name List of Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Divine Name List|lt_ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tagata District|ja|田方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yanagihara Shrine Myōjin Taisha", listing it as the only {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tagata District.From "Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture", Shimokounuki Village section.Note: Although printed editions list this shrine as Myōjin Taisha, the Kujō family manuscripts designate the Iwayo-no-mikoto Shrine as Myōjin Taisha instead (see "History of Shizuoka Prefecture" [Yamakawa Publishing] p. 50). Also, the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' contains an entry "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin". However, since the left bank of the Kano River—including both the present shrine grounds and the former site (Yanagihara in Numazu City)—has belonged to Suruga Province at least since the early modern period, doubts have been raised regarding its classification as Myōjin Taisha. Regarding this, given that the place name "Yanagihara" has persisted and numerous archaeological finds from the surrounding area indicate the reasonable enshrinement of a Myōjin Taisha here, there is a theory that a border shift between Suruga and Izu occurred after the Engishiki. In the past, the parish area comprised Uekounuki Village, Shimokounuki Village, Zentaifu, and Wareidō, and the shrine was also known as "Ōmiya Daimyōjin", "Kounuki Daimyōjin", and "Matsuhiko Daimyōjin". During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Ooasa Shrine (Shimokounuki, Numazu City)
Possibly the mountain shrine of Yanagihara Shrine.]] ; Ooasa Shrine :* Location: Numazu City, Shimokounuki, Yamamiya-mae 3056-2 ({{Coord|35|04|25.83|N|138|51|52.74|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Ooasa Shrine (possibly mountain shrine)}}) :* Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ōhirume-no-mikoto|ja|大日孁命|lt=Great Sun Goddess|lt_ja|おおひるめのみこと|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ooasa Shrine" branch shrine, formerly {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Annual Festival: January 16 : It is known as "Ooasa Shrine". It is located approximately 1.5 km southwest of Yanagihara Shrine at the southern foot of Ushinobeyama facing the sea. It shares the same enshrinement tradition and content as Yanagihara Shrine, and is considered to be one entity. It is speculated that there once was a shoal between it and Yanagihara Shrine, and that this shrine was originally enshrined on a small island in the sea as a mountain shrine. Furthermore, the designated branch shrine of the Shikinaisha "Ooasa Shrine" is not this shrine but rather {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine (Izu City)|ja|大宮神社 (伊豆市)|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt_ja|大宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Kamishiroiwa, Izu City), which is more strongly regarded.See "The Gods of Japan", section on Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|
{{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatanamachi)
One theory identifies it as Izu Province's Miya. There are various theories regarding its relationship with Yanagihara Shrine of Numazu.]] ; {{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Location: {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kitatanamachi 4-7 ({{Coord|35|7|4.80|N|138|55|5.84|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Yanagihara Shrine (Possibly the remote worship office of the former provincial capital)}}) :* Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: Either as recorded in the Kami Ranking List as "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin" or as the Izu Province's Miya{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=559}} : It is known as "Yanagihara Shrine". It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (December 31) == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shimokounuki Miwaku 335 '''Transportation Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company (JR Central)|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central) {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Gotemba Line|ja|御殿場線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Numazu Station|ja|沼津駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes on foot) ** Bus and rental bicycle available == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{reflist|group=注}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Records * {{Cite book|和書|editor=秋山章纂修、萩原正平増訂|author=|year=|title=増訂豆州志稿 巻八上|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|増訂豆州志稿 巻八上}}}} ** 東海|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022208|ref={{Harvid|日本の神々|1987}}}} == Related Topics == * {{commonscat-inline|Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu)}} == External Links == * {{otheruses|Shinto Shrine in Numazu, Shizuoka Prefecture|Shinto Shrine in Mishima, Shizuoka Prefecture|Yanagihara Shrine (Mishima City)}} {{Shinto Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) keidai.JPG|260px]]
Main Shrine Worship Hall (left) and Sessha Yoshida Shrine (right) |Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miwaku 335 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|8.1|N|138|52|16.4|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=楊原神社}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Formerly {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = |Fuda and such = |Annual Festival = {{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka#Japan|label=楊原神社|lat_deg=35|lat_min=5|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=138|lon_min=52|lon_sec=16.4|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=220|caption=}} {{Infobox mapframe|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-width=220}} }} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Torii
]] '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja|旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == '''Principal Enshrined Deity''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ōyamatsumi-kami)Deity information is based on the shrine's historical records. '''Auxiliary Enshrined Deities''' * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Stone-Long Princess Deity|lt_ja|石長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwanagahime-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Tree-Flower Blooming Princess Deity|lt_ja|木花開耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto) == History == === Overview === The founding period is unknown. Prior to its relocation to the current site during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is said that the shrine was enshrined at the location of Yanagihara in Numazu City (approximately 500 m to the southeast of the present site).Shrine historical records. The earliest mention in national history appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', which records that on January 17 of the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade accompanied by a bestowment of a certain {{ill|Kami Ranking|ja|神階|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' {{ill|Divine Name List of Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Divine Name List|lt_ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tagata District|ja|田方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yanagihara Shrine Myōjin Taisha", listing it as the only {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tagata District.From "Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture", Shimokounuki Village section.Note: Although printed editions list this shrine as Myōjin Taisha, the Kujō family manuscripts designate the Iwayo-no-mikoto Shrine as Myōjin Taisha instead (see "History of Shizuoka Prefecture" [Yamakawa Publishing] p. 50). Also, the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' contains an entry "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin". However, since the left bank of the Kano River—including both the present shrine grounds and the former site (Yanagihara in Numazu City)—has belonged to Suruga Province at least since the early modern period, doubts have been raised regarding its classification as Myōjin Taisha. Regarding this, given that the place name "Yanagihara" has persisted and numerous archaeological finds from the surrounding area indicate the reasonable enshrinement of a Myōjin Taisha here, there is a theory that a border shift between Suruga and Izu occurred after the Engishiki. In the past, the parish area comprised Uekounuki Village, Shimokounuki Village, Zentaifu, and Wareidō, and the shrine was also known as "Ōmiya Daimyōjin", "Kounuki Daimyōjin", and "Matsuhiko Daimyōjin". During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Ooasa Shrine (Shimokounuki, Numazu City)
Possibly the mountain shrine of Yanagihara Shrine.]] ; Ooasa Shrine :* Location: Numazu City, Shimokounuki, Yamamiya-mae 3056-2 ({{Coord|35|04|25.83|N|138|51|52.74|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Ooasa Shrine (possibly mountain shrine)}}) :* Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ōhirume-no-mikoto|ja|大日孁命|lt=Great Sun Goddess|lt_ja|おおひるめのみこと|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ooasa Shrine" branch shrine, formerly {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Annual Festival: January 16 : It is known as "Ooasa Shrine". It is located approximately 1.5 km southwest of Yanagihara Shrine at the southern foot of Ushinobeyama facing the sea. It shares the same enshrinement tradition and content as Yanagihara Shrine, and is considered to be one entity. It is speculated that there once was a shoal between it and Yanagihara Shrine, and that this shrine was originally enshrined on a small island in the sea as a mountain shrine. Furthermore, the designated branch shrine of the Shikinaisha "Ooasa Shrine" is not this shrine but rather {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine (Izu City)|ja|大宮神社 (伊豆市)|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt_ja|大宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Kamishiroiwa, Izu City), which is more strongly regarded.See "The Gods of Japan", section on Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|
{{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatanamachi)
One theory identifies it as Izu Province's Miya. There are various theories regarding its relationship with Yanagihara Shrine of Numazu.]] ; {{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Location: {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kitatanamachi 4-7 ({{Coord|35|7|4.80|N|138|55|5.84|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Yanagihara Shrine (Possibly the remote worship office of the former provincial capital)}}) :* Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: Either as recorded in the Kami Ranking List as "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin" or as the Izu Province's Miya{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=559}} : It is known as "Yanagihara Shrine". It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (December 31) == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shimokounuki Miwaku 335 '''Transportation Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company (JR Central)|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central) {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Gotemba Line|ja|御殿場線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Numazu Station|ja|沼津駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes on foot) ** Bus and rental bicycle available {{otheruses|Shinto Shrine in Numazu, Shizuoka Prefecture|Shinto Shrine in Mishima, Shizuoka Prefecture|Yanagihara Shrine (Mishima City)}} {{Shinto Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) keidai.JPG|260px]]
Main Shrine Worship Hall (left) and Sessha Yoshida Shrine (right) |Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miwaku 335 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|8.1|N|138|52|16.4|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=楊原神社}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Formerly {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = |Fuda and such = |Annual Festival = {{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka#Japan|label=楊原神社|lat_deg=35|lat_min=5|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=138|lon_min=52|lon_sec=16.4|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=220|caption=}} {{Infobox mapframe|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-width=220}} }} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Torii
]] '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja|旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == '''Principal Enshrined Deity''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ōyamatsumi-kami)Deity information is based on the shrine's historical records. '''Auxiliary Enshrined Deities''' * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Stone-Long Princess Deity|lt_ja|石長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwanagahime-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Tree-Flower Blooming Princess Deity|lt_ja|木花開耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto) == History == === Overview === The founding period is unknown. Prior to its relocation to the current site during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is said that the shrine was enshrined at the location of Yanagihara in Numazu City (approximately 500 m to the southeast of the present site).Shrine historical records. The earliest mention in national history appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', which records that on January 17 of the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade accompanied by a bestowment of a certain {{ill|Kami Ranking|ja|神階|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' {{ill|Divine Name List of Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Divine Name List|lt_ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tagata District|ja|田方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yanagihara Shrine Myōjin Taisha", listing it as the only {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tagata District.From "Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture", Shimokounuki Village section.Note: Although printed editions list this shrine as Myōjin Taisha, the Kujō family manuscripts designate the Iwayo-no-mikoto Shrine as Myōjin Taisha instead (see "History of Shizuoka Prefecture" [Yamakawa Publishing] p. 50). Also, the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' contains an entry "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin". However, since the left bank of the Kano River—including both the present shrine grounds and the former site (Yanagihara in Numazu City)—has belonged to Suruga Province at least since the early modern period, doubts have been raised regarding its classification as Myōjin Taisha. Regarding this, given that the place name "Yanagihara" has persisted and numerous archaeological finds from the surrounding area indicate the reasonable enshrinement of a Myōjin Taisha here, there is a theory that a border shift between Suruga and Izu occurred after the Engishiki. In the past, the parish area comprised Uekounuki Village, Shimokounuki Village, Zentaifu, and Wareidō, and the shrine was also known as "Ōmiya Daimyōjin", "Kounuki Daimyōjin", and "Matsuhiko Daimyōjin". During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Ooasa Shrine (Shimokounuki, Numazu City)
Possibly the mountain shrine of Yanagihara Shrine.]] ; Ooasa Shrine :* Location: Numazu City, Shimokounuki, Yamamiya-mae 3056-2 ({{Coord|35|04|25.83|N|138|51|52.74|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Ooasa Shrine (possibly mountain shrine)}}) :* Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ōhirume-no-mikoto|ja|大日孁命|lt=Great Sun Goddess|lt_ja|おおひるめのみこと|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ooasa Shrine" branch shrine, formerly {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Annual Festival: January 16 : It is known as "Ooasa Shrine". It is located approximately 1.5 km southwest of Yanagihara Shrine at the southern foot of Ushinobeyama facing the sea. It shares the same enshrinement tradition and content as Yanagihara Shrine, and is considered to be one entity. It is speculated that there once was a shoal between it and Yanagihara Shrine, and that this shrine was originally enshrined on a small island in the sea as a mountain shrine. Furthermore, the designated branch shrine of the Shikinaisha "Ooasa Shrine" is not this shrine but rather {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine (Izu City)|ja|大宮神社 (伊豆市)|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt_ja|大宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Kamishiroiwa, Izu City), which is more strongly regarded.See "The Gods of Japan", section on Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|
{{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatanamachi)
One theory identifies it as Izu Province's Miya. There are various theories regarding its relationship with Yanagihara Shrine of Numazu.]] ; {{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Location: {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kitatanamachi 4-7 ({{Coord|35|7|4.80|N|138|55|5.84|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Yanagihara Shrine (Possibly the remote worship office of the former provincial capital)}}) :* Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: Either as recorded in the Kami Ranking List as "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin" or as the Izu Province's Miya{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=559}} : It is known as "Yanagihara Shrine". It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * Main Shrine Worship Hall (left) and Sessha Yoshida Shrine (right) |Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miwaku 335 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|8.1|N|138|52|16.4|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=楊原神社}} |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Formerly {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = |Fuda and such = |Annual Festival = {{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka#Japan|label=楊原神社|lat_deg=35|lat_min=5|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=138|lon_min=52|lon_sec=16.4|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=220|caption=}} {{Infobox mapframe|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-width=220}} }} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Torii
]] '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja|旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == '''Principal Enshrined Deity''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ōyamatsumi-kami)Deity information is based on the shrine's historical records. '''Auxiliary Enshrined Deities''' * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Stone-Long Princess Deity|lt_ja|石長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwanagahime-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Tree-Flower Blooming Princess Deity|lt_ja|木花開耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto) == History == === Overview === The founding period is unknown. Prior to its relocation to the current site during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is said that the shrine was enshrined at the location of Yanagihara in Numazu City (approximately 500 m to the southeast of the present site).Shrine historical records. The earliest mention in national history appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', which records that on January 17 of the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade accompanied by a bestowment of a certain {{ill|Kami Ranking|ja|神階|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' {{ill|Divine Name List of Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Divine Name List|lt_ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tagata District|ja|田方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yanagihara Shrine Myōjin Taisha", listing it as the only {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tagata District.From "Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture", Shimokounuki Village section.Note: Although printed editions list this shrine as Myōjin Taisha, the Kujō family manuscripts designate the Iwayo-no-mikoto Shrine as Myōjin Taisha instead (see "History of Shizuoka Prefecture" [Yamakawa Publishing] p. 50). Also, the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' contains an entry "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin". However, since the left bank of the Kano River—including both the present shrine grounds and the former site (Yanagihara in Numazu City)—has belonged to Suruga Province at least since the early modern period, doubts have been raised regarding its classification as Myōjin Taisha. Regarding this, given that the place name "Yanagihara" has persisted and numerous archaeological finds from the surrounding area indicate the reasonable enshrinement of a Myōjin Taisha here, there is a theory that a border shift between Suruga and Izu occurred after the Engishiki. In the past, the parish area comprised Uekounuki Village, Shimokounuki Village, Zentaifu, and Wareidō, and the shrine was also known as "Ōmiya Daimyōjin", "Kounuki Daimyōjin", and "Matsuhiko Daimyōjin". During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Ooasa Shrine (Shimokounuki, Numazu City)
Possibly the mountain shrine of Yanagihara Shrine.]] ; Ooasa Shrine :* Location: Numazu City, Shimokounuki, Yamamiya-mae 3056-2 ({{Coord|35|04|25.83|N|138|51|52.74|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Ooasa Shrine (possibly mountain shrine)}}) :* Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ōhirume-no-mikoto|ja|大日孁命|lt=Great Sun Goddess|lt_ja|おおひるめのみこと|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ooasa Shrine" branch shrine, formerly {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Annual Festival: January 16 : It is known as "Ooasa Shrine". It is located approximately 1.5 km southwest of Yanagihara Shrine at the southern foot of Ushinobeyama facing the sea. It shares the same enshrinement tradition and content as Yanagihara Shrine, and is considered to be one entity. It is speculated that there once was a shoal between it and Yanagihara Shrine, and that this shrine was originally enshrined on a small island in the sea as a mountain shrine. Furthermore, the designated branch shrine of the Shikinaisha "Ooasa Shrine" is not this shrine but rather {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine (Izu City)|ja|大宮神社 (伊豆市)|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt_ja|大宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Kamishiroiwa, Izu City), which is more strongly regarded.See "The Gods of Japan", section on Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|
{{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatanamachi)
One theory identifies it as Izu Province's Miya. There are various theories regarding its relationship with Yanagihara Shrine of Numazu.]] ; {{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Location: {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kitatanamachi 4-7 ({{Coord|35|7|4.80|N|138|55|5.84|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Yanagihara Shrine (Possibly the remote worship office of the former provincial capital)}}) :* Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: Either as recorded in the Kami Ranking List as "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin" or as the Izu Province's Miya{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=559}} : It is known as "Yanagihara Shrine". It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (December 31) == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shimokounuki Miwaku 335 '''Transportation Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company (JR Central)|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Central) {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Gotemba Line|ja|御殿場線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Numazu Station|ja|沼津駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes on foot) ** Bus and rental bicycle available == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{reflist|group=注}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Records * {{Cite book|和書|editor=秋山章纂修、萩原正平増訂|author=|year=|title=増訂豆州志稿 巻八上|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|増訂豆州志稿 巻八上}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf 'Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1']}} (refer to Waseda University Library). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=Yanagihara Shrine|title=[[Prefectural and Village Shrines Meiji Shrine Materials]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/901}} 'Meiji Shrine Materials: Prefectural and Village Shrines, Volume 1'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection) – refer to panels 901–902. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=三島市誌編纂委員会編|author=|year=1983|title=三島市誌 下|publisher=三島市|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|三島市誌 下|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimokounuki Village|title=日本歴史地名体系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|静岡県の地名|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[谷川健一]]編|author=神野善治|year=1987|chapter=Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022208|ref={{Harvid|日本の神々|1987}}}} == Related Topics == * {{commonscat-inline|Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu)}} == External Links == * During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
Ooasa Shrine (Shimokounuki, Numazu City)
Possibly the mountain shrine of Yanagihara Shrine.]] ; Ooasa Shrine :* Location: Numazu City, Shimokounuki, Yamamiya-mae 3056-2 ({{Coord|35|04|25.83|N|138|51|52.74|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Ooasa Shrine (possibly mountain shrine)}}) :* Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ōhirume-no-mikoto|ja|大日孁命|lt=Great Sun Goddess|lt_ja|おおひるめのみこと|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Ooasa Shrine" branch shrine, formerly {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Annual Festival: January 16 : It is known as "Ooasa Shrine". It is located approximately 1.5 km southwest of Yanagihara Shrine at the southern foot of Ushinobeyama facing the sea. It shares the same enshrinement tradition and content as Yanagihara Shrine, and is considered to be one entity. It is speculated that there once was a shoal between it and Yanagihara Shrine, and that this shrine was originally enshrined on a small island in the sea as a mountain shrine. Furthermore, the designated branch shrine of the Shikinaisha "Ooasa Shrine" is not this shrine but rather {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine (Izu City)|ja|大宮神社 (伊豆市)|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt_ja|大宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Kamishiroiwa, Izu City), which is more strongly regarded.See "The Gods of Japan", section on Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|
{{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatanamachi)
One theory identifies it as Izu Province's Miya. There are various theories regarding its relationship with Yanagihara Shrine of Numazu.]] ; {{ill|Mishima Taisha#Former Auxiliary Shrine|ja|三嶋大社#元摂末社|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt_ja|楊原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Location: {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kitatanamachi 4-7 ({{Coord|35|7|4.80|N|138|55|5.84|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|name=Associated Shrine: Yanagihara Shrine (Possibly the remote worship office of the former provincial capital)}}) :* Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Shrine Rank: Either as recorded in the Kami Ranking List as "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin" or as the Izu Province's Miya{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=559}} : It is known as "Yanagihara Shrine". It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * Torii
]] '''Yanagihara Shrine''' (やなぎはらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimokounuki|ja|下香貫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja|旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == '''Principal Enshrined Deity''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja|大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ōyamatsumi-kami)Deity information is based on the shrine's historical records. '''Auxiliary Enshrined Deities''' * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Stone-Long Princess Deity|lt_ja|石長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwanagahime-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Tree-Flower Blooming Princess Deity|lt_ja|木花開耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto) == History == === Overview === The founding period is unknown. Prior to its relocation to the current site during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is said that the shrine was enshrined at the location of Yanagihara in Numazu City (approximately 500 m to the southeast of the present site).Shrine historical records. The earliest mention in national history appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', which records that on January 17 of the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade accompanied by a bestowment of a certain {{ill|Kami Ranking|ja|神階|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' {{ill|Divine Name List of Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Divine Name List|lt_ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in {{ill|Izu Province|ja|伊豆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tagata District|ja|田方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yanagihara Shrine Myōjin Taisha", listing it as the only {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tagata District.From "Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture", Shimokounuki Village section.Note: Although printed editions list this shrine as Myōjin Taisha, the Kujō family manuscripts designate the Iwayo-no-mikoto Shrine as Myōjin Taisha instead (see "History of Shizuoka Prefecture" [Yamakawa Publishing] p. 50). Also, the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' contains an entry "Junior First Rank, Yakihara no Myōjin". However, since the left bank of the Kano River—including both the present shrine grounds and the former site (Yanagihara in Numazu City)—has belonged to Suruga Province at least since the early modern period, doubts have been raised regarding its classification as Myōjin Taisha. Regarding this, given that the place name "Yanagihara" has persisted and numerous archaeological finds from the surrounding area indicate the reasonable enshrinement of a Myōjin Taisha here, there is a theory that a border shift between Suruga and Izu occurred after the Engishiki. In the past, the parish area comprised Uekounuki Village, Shimokounuki Village, Zentaifu, and Wareidō, and the shrine was also known as "Ōmiya Daimyōjin", "Kounuki Daimyōjin", and "Matsuhiko Daimyōjin". During the Sengoku period, the shrine buildings and other structures were burned down amid conflicts involving the Hōjō and Takeda clans, and in the 18th year of the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1590|ja|1590th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt and relocated from the site of Yanagihara to its present location. It is said that the shrine once possessed a fief of 500 koku, 8 priestly establishments, and over 30 shrine official families ({{ill|Shrine Clan|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the 8th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February {{ill|1875|ja|1875th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kami Ranking === * A record in the Rokukokushi concerning the bestowal of a kami Rank upon "Yanagihara God": ** In the first year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan era|lt_ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on January 27, the Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). ** In the 12th year of the Jōgan era ({{ill|870|ja|870th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on May 29, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade was conferred (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). ** In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Ninwa era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|886|ja|886年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on November 25, the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade was granted (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). * Junior First Rank (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Izu Province Kami Ranking List|ja|伊豆国神階帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'') – the notation is "Yakihara no Myōjin". == Shrine Grounds == File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall == Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines == === Sessha (Auxiliary Shrines) === ; Yoshida ShrineAccording to the shrine's historical records. :* Annual Festival: October 14 : It is enshrined to the right of the main shrine. During an epidemic in the 5th year of the {{ill|Ansei era|ja|安政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1858|ja|1858th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was invited from the {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|ja|吉田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto to ward off the plague. Initially, it was enshrined at Sojigae-no-mine in Shimokounuki, but in the 28th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated to the shrine grounds. === Massha (Subordinate Shrines) === * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hodabetsu-no-mikoto|lt_ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sukunabikona Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|lt_ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tsushima Shrine – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Associated Shrines === [[File:Ooasa-jinja (Numazu) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|

It is the former auxiliary shrine of Mishima Taisha. There is a theory that it served as a worship office from the former provincial capital in Mishima to remotely venerate Yanagihara Shrine in Numazu{{Sfn|Revised Dōshū Shikō, Volume 8, Part 1|p=36}}, and some say that the Kami Ranking List refers to this shrine. Another theory posits that, after the original site in Numazu was incorporated into Suruga Province, the shrine was relocated to Nakajima, and later, when Tokugawa Iemitsu established his palace in that area, it was moved to the present location in Kitatanamachi, Mishima City{{Sfn|Mishima City Chronicles, Lower Volume|1988|p=560}}. Its annual festival is held on July 5. == Rituals and Festivals == * New Year's Festival (January 1)Festival details are based on the shrine's historical records. * Annual Grand Festival :* The annual festival is jointly held by Yanagihara Shrine and Ooasa Shrine. The {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of both shrines are carried to Wareidōhama near Ooasa Shrine, and a ritual known as the "Mikoshi Washing Ceremony", in which the mikoshi is carried into the sea, is performed. *:* Mikoshi Washing Ceremony (January 15) *:* Ooasa Shrine Annual Festival (January 16) *:* Yanagihara Shrine Annual Festival (January 17) * National Foundation Festival (February 11) * Spring Grand Festival (March 16) * Ōharae (Rite of Purification) (June 30) * Yoshida Shrine Annual Festival (October 14) * Niiname-sai (New Rice Harvest Thanksgiving Festival) (November 20) * {{otheruses|Shinto Shrine in Numazu, Shizuoka Prefecture|Shinto Shrine in Mishima, Shizuoka Prefecture|Yanagihara Shrine (Mishima City)}} {{Shinto Shrine |Name = Yanagihara Shrine |image = [[File:Yanagihara-jinja (Numazu) keidai.JPG|260px]]
